LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT TRITEC AMERICAS, LLC'S MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION. THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. THE COURT RULES AS FOLLOWS: THE MOTION IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO REFILING UPON A FULLER & DETAILED SHOWING OF LIKELIHOOD OF SUCCESS. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: REYES HUNTER CSR #6576 EMAIL: RHUNTER@SFTC.ORG

calendar.matters:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Dec-14-2017 Off Calendar

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Nov-02-2017 Continued To Dec-19-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Defendantcross-Complainant Tritec Americas, Llcs Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof; Declaration Of Joshua Bordin-Wosk
Off Calendar Oct-10-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Benjamin Sampson, Esq. of Bordin Martorell LLP, Tel. 323.457.2110, for defendant and moving party Tritec Americas, LLC;
David Houska, Esq. of Cooley, Tel. 415.693.2153, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: At the start of the hearing, the Court orally issued its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT TRITEC AMERICAS, LLC's Motion For Preliminary Injunction. The matter was argued. At the conclusion of arguments, the court adopted its tentative ruling. The motion is denied without prejudice to refiling upon a fuller & detailed showing of likelihood of success. Prevailing party to prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Reyes Hunter CSR #6576 email: rhunter@sftc.org
